Name: CISA Secure Cloud Business Applications (SCuBA) Google Workspace Baselines
Description: The CISA Secure Cloud Business Applications (SCuBA) project provides security configuration baselines for Google Workspace. These baselines define minimum security requirements using RFC 2119 requirement levels (SHALL/SHOULD/SHALL NOT) and include mappings to NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 and MITRE ATT&CK.

Requirements.Description: Phishing-resistant MFA SHALL be required for all users
If phishing-resistant MFA is not yet tenable, an MFA method from the list of acceptable MFA methods SHALL be used as an interim solution
If phishing-resistant MFA is not yet tenable, SMS or Voice as the MFA method SHALL NOT be used
The 2SV enrollment period for new users SHALL be set to 1 day to 1 week
Allow user to trust the device SHALL be disabled
Context-Aware Access device-based policies SHOULD be implemented
Post-SSO verification for corporate SSO profile SHOULD be enabled
Post-SSO verification for other SSO profiles SHOULD be enabled
Google Workspace sessions SHALL re-authenticate after 12 hours
Password strength SHALL be enforced
Minimum password length SHALL be at least 12 characters
Minimum password length SHOULD be at least 15 characters
Password policy SHALL be enforced at next sign-in
Password reuse SHALL be restricted
Password expiration period SHALL NOT be set
All admin accounts SHALL be cloud-only and not federated
Between 2 and 8 super admin users SHALL be configured
Unmanaged conflicting accounts SHOULD be replaced with managed ones
Account recovery for super admins SHALL be disabled
Account recovery for non-super admin users SHALL be disabled
Adding a recovery phone number and email address SHOULD be disabled
Privileged accounts SHALL be enrolled in the Advanced Protection Program
Sensitive user accounts SHOULD be enrolled in the Advanced Protection Program
Unconfigured third-party apps SHALL be restricted from accessing GWS services
Third-party apps that do not meet minimum access requirements SHALL be blocked
Access to high-risk scopes SHALL be blocked unless explicitly trusted
OAuth apps with domain-wide delegation SHALL be reviewed periodically
Internal apps SHALL be allowed to access restricted Google Workspace APIs
Only approved Marketplace apps SHALL be allowed for installation
Google Takeout SHALL be disabled for users
All system-defined alerting rules SHALL be enabled with alerts sent to admin email addresses
Google Workspace logs SHALL be sent to the organization's SIEM
Google Workspace logs SHALL be stored for a minimum of 6 months in active storage
Data SHALL be stored in the United States
Supplemental data storage SHALL be set to the United States
Non-essential additional Google services SHALL be disabled for all users
Essential additional Google services SHALL be enabled only for authorized users
Multiple super admins SHOULD be required to approve sensitive admin actions
A DLP policy SHALL be configured for Drive
A DLP policy SHALL be configured for Gmail
A DLP policy SHALL be configured for Chat
The DLP rule severity level SHALL be configured appropriately for the organization
Mail Delegation SHOULD be disabled
DKIM SHOULD be enabled for all domains
An SPF policy SHALL be published for each domain that fails all non-approved senders
A DMARC policy SHALL be published at the full domain or the second-level domain for all Google Workspace domains, including user alias domains

Attributes.SubSection: 4 Domain-based Message Authentication, Reporting, and Conformance

Requirements.Description: The DMARC message rejection option SHALL be p=reject

Attributes.SubSection: 4 Domain-based Message Authentication, Reporting, and Conformance

Requirements.Description: The DMARC point of contact for aggregate reports SHALL include reports@dmarc.cyber.dhs.gov

Attributes.SubSection: 4 Domain-based Message Authentication, Reporting, and Conformance

Requirements.Description: An agency point of contact SHOULD be included for aggregate and failure reports

Attributes.SubSection: 4 Domain-based Message Authentication, Reporting, and Conformance

Requirements.Description: Protect against encrypted attachments from untrusted senders SHALL be enabled
Protect against attachments with scripts from untrusted senders SHALL be enabled
Protect against anomalous attachment types in emails SHALL be enabled
Google SHOULD be allowed to automatically apply future recommended settings for attachments
Emails flagged by SCuBA policies GWS.GMAIL.5.1 through GWS.GMAIL.5.3 SHALL NOT be kept in inbox
Any third-party or outside application selected for attachment protection SHOULD offer services comparable to those offered by Google Workspace (GWS)
Identify links behind shortened URLs SHALL be enabled
Scan linked images SHALL be enabled
Show warning prompt for any click on links to untrusted domains SHALL be enabled
Google SHALL be allowed to automatically apply future recommended settings for links and external images
Any third-party or outside application selected for links and external images protection SHOULD offer services comparable to those offered by Google Workspace (GWS)
Protect against domain spoofing based on similar domain names SHALL be enabled
Protect against spoofing of employee names SHALL be enabled
Protect against inbound emails spoofing your domain SHALL be enabled
Protect against any unauthenticated emails SHALL be enabled
Protect your Groups from inbound emails spoofing your domain SHALL be enabled
Emails flagged by SCuBA policies GWS.GMAIL.7.1 through GWS.GMAIL.7.5 SHALL NOT be kept in inbox
Google SHALL be allowed to automatically apply future recommended settings for spoofing and authentication
Any third-party or outside application selected for spoofing and authentication protection SHOULD offer services comparable to those offered by Google Workspace
User email uploads SHALL be disabled to protect against unauthorized files being introduced into the secured environment
POP and IMAP access SHALL be disabled to protect sensitive agency or organization emails from being accessed through legacy applications or other third-party mail clients

Attributes.SubSection: 9 POP and IMAP Access for Users

Requirements.Description: Google Workspace Sync SHOULD be disabled
Automatic forwarding SHOULD be disabled, especially to external domains
Using a per-user outbound gateway that is a mail server other than the Google Workspace (GWS) mail servers SHALL be disabled
Unintended external reply warnings SHALL be enabled
An email allowlist SHOULD not be implemented
Enhanced pre-delivery message scanning SHALL be enabled to prevent phishing
Any third-party or outside application selected for enhanced pre-delivery message scanning SHOULD offer services comparable to those offered by Google Workspace
Security sandbox SHOULD be enabled to provide additional protections for emails
Any third-party or outside application selected for security sandbox SHOULD offer services comparable to those offered by Google Workspace
Comprehensive mail storage SHOULD be enabled to allow information traceability across applications
Domains SHALL NOT be added to lists that bypass spam filters
Domains SHALL NOT be added to lists that bypass spam filters and hide warnings
Bypass spam filters and hide warnings for all messages from internal and external senders SHALL NOT be enabled
External sharing SHALL be restricted to allowlisted domains
Receiving files from outside of allowlisted domains SHOULD be disabled
Warnings SHALL be enabled when a user is attempting to share with someone not in allowlisted domains
If sharing outside of the organization, then agencies SHOULD disable sharing of files with individuals who are not using a Google account
Any OUs that do allow external sharing SHOULD disable making content available to anyone with the link
Agencies SHALL set access checking to recipients only
Users SHOULD NOT be allowed to upload or move content to shared drives owned by another organization
Private to owner SHALL be the default access level for newly created items
Out-of-Domain file-level warnings SHALL be enabled
If external sharing is not allowed, then forms owned by users within the organization SHOULD NOT be able to accept responses from anyone with the link outside the organization
If receiving external files is not allowed, then users in the organization SHOULD NOT be able to submit responses to forms from users or shared drives outside of the organization
Agencies SHOULD NOT allow members with manager access to override shared Google Drive creation settings
Agencies SHALL allow users who are not shared Google Drive members to be added to files
Agencies SHALL enable the security update for Google Drive files
Agencies SHOULD disable Google Drive SDK access
Agencies SHALL disable Google Drive Add-Ons

Attributes.SubSection: 5 User Installation of Drive and Docs Add-Ons

Requirements.Description: Google Drive for Desktop SHALL be enabled only for authorized devices
External Sharing Options for Primary Calendars SHALL be configured to Only free/busy information (hide event details)
External sharing options for secondary calendars SHALL be configured to Only free/busy information (hide event details)
External invitations warnings SHALL be enabled to prompt users before sending invitations
Calendar Interop SHOULD be disabled

Microsoft 365 (Graph API) SHALL be used in lieu of basic authentication to establish connectivity between tenants or organizations in cases where Calendar Interop is deemed necessary for agency mission fulfillment
Appointment Schedule with Payments SHALL be disabled
Chat history SHALL be enabled for information traceability
Users SHALL NOT be allowed to change their history setting
External file sharing SHALL be disabled to protect sensitive information from unauthorized or accidental sharing
Space history SHOULD be enabled for information traceability
External chat messaging SHALL be restricted to allowlisted domains only
Chat content reporting SHALL be enabled for all conversation types
All reporting message categories SHOULD be selected
External users who were not explicitly invited SHALL be required to ask to join
Meeting access SHALL be disabled for meetings created by users who are not members of any Google Workspace (GWS) tenant or organization
Host Management meeting features SHALL be enabled
Warn for external participants SHALL be enabled
Incoming calls SHALL be restricted to contacts and other users in the organization
Automatic recordings for Google Meet SHALL be disabled
Automatic transcripts for Google Meet SHALL be disabled
Group access from outside the organization SHALL be disabled unless explicitly granted by the group owner
Group owners' ability to add external members to groups SHOULD be disabled unless necessary for agency mission fulfillment
Group owners' ability to allow posting to a group by an external, non-group member SHOULD be disabled unless necessary for agency mission fulfillment
Group creation SHOULD be restricted to admins within the organization unless necessary for agency mission fulfillment
The default permission to view conversations SHOULD be set to All Group Members
The Ability for Groups to be Hidden from the Directory SHALL be disabled

Attributes.SubSection: 4 Ability to Hide Groups from the Directory

Requirements.Description: Sites Service SHOULD be disabled for all users
Who can join classes in your domain SHALL be restricted to users in your domain or allowlisted domains
Which classes users in your domain can join SHALL be restricted to classes in your domain or allowlisted domains
Users SHALL NOT be able to authorize apps to access their Google Classroom data
Roster import with Clever SHOULD be turned off
Only teachers SHALL be allowed to unenroll students from classes
Class creation SHALL be restricted to verified teachers only
